ORDER AND STIPULATION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDMENT TO COMPLAINT TO SUBSTITUTE TRUE NAME OF "DOE" DEFENDANT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 474
AMENDMENT TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT PER ORDER DEFENDANT SUED HEREIN AS DOE 1 TO BE PACIFIC INPATIENT MEDICAL GROUP, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS AND SONIA PASALO, L.V.N. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS IT RELATES TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND THROUGH EIGHTH, TENTH, AND ELEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS IT RELATES TO PLAINTIFF'S NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE PENAL CODE 368 DOES NOT PROVIDE A CIVIL CAUSE OF ACTION AND IT IS UNCLEAR FROM PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IS ASSERTED AS NEGLIGENCE PER SE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - DEFENDANTS SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS AND SONIA PASALO, L.V.N.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF'S PRAYER FOR RELIEF AS IT RELATES TO THEIR SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION IMPROPERLY INCLUDES A REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES UNDER PLAINTIFF'S THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS MUST FIRST OBTAIN A COURT ORDER TO AMEND THEIR COMPLAINT TO REQUEST PUNITIVE DAMAGES UNDER THEIR THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.13. THE REMAINDER OF PLAINTIFF'S PRAYER FOR RELIEF CONFORMS TO STATUTORY REQUIREMENTS AND DEFENDANT CITES NO AUTHORITY SUPPORTING ITS CONTENTION THAT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC HEALTH INVESTIGATIONS AND CITATIONS ARE INADMISSIBLE IN PLEADINGS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

ORDER RE: DEFENDANTS SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS "DBA" CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER'S AND SONIA A. PASALO, L.V.N.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN THE ALTERNATIVE

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JOSEPH FEE, LAURA REES, AND NANCY FEE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT SEEKING PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GRANTED. THE MOTION WAS TIMELY FILED UNDER CCP 425.13 AND PLAINTIFFS HAVE ADDUCED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THEIR REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE COURT DOES NOT WEIGH THE EVIDENCE. (COLLEGE HOSPITAL INC. V. SUP. CRT. (1994) 8 CAL.4TH 704, 719, N.6.) PLAINTIFF CAN AMEND TO SEEK PUNITIVES ON THE IIED/ELDER ABUSE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF CANNOT AMEND TO SEEK PUNITIVES ON THE FRAUD/MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT DENIED THE MOTION TO AMEND TO ALLEGE PUNITIVE DAMAGE ON THE INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION AGAINST SWBH AND DENIED THE MOTION TO AMEND TO ALLEGE CONCEALMENT AGAINST SWBH AND GRANT THE REST OF THE MOTION. THE MOTION TO AMEND TO ALLEGE PUNITIVE DAMAGE ON THE INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION AGAINST MISS PASALO IS GRANTED AND DENIED AS TO CONCEALMENT AS TO MISS PASALO. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR # 11332 =302/HK

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Experts Depositions
Off Calendar May-02-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JESSICA T. SIZEMORE, TEL. NO. 1-619-237-3490 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. JOHN H. GOMEZ OF GOMEZ TRIAL ATTORNEYS, TEL. NO. 1-619-237-3490 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. KATHRYN B. MEADOWS, TEL. NO. 1-619-231-9460 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. CYRUS A. TABARI OF THE SHEURMAN, MARTIN & GARVIN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-408-288-9700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS;
ATTY. G. PATRICK GALLOWAY OF THE GALLOWAY LUCCHESE & PICCHI, TEL. NO. 1-925-930-9090 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SONIA PASALO.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs' motion to compel deposition of the defendants' experts is granted. Substantial justice is not served by precluding plaintiffs from deposing defendants' experts due to their counsel's failure to timely notice the depositions. In a case of this nature it is extremely disadvantageous, and contrary to the spirit of the discovery statutes, for the plaintiffs to be deprived of the ability to take the depositions of defendants' experts. While it may be that such a disadvantage should be allowed to stand in some cases, where, as here, the mere failure to timely notice the depositions is not such a case. Defendants must make all their experts available for depositions at the earliest possible dates that the experts are available, if at all possible prior to the current trial date of May 15, 2017. If it is not possible for all the depositions to occur before May 15, 2017 and/or the defendants wish a short continuance of the trial because they need to devote much of the two weeks before trial for these depositions, the trial will be continued to May 30, 2017 at 9:30am in department 206 or any later date that is agreeable to all the parties. The continuance of the trial, if needed or requested by defendants, remedies any prejudice that defendants might suffer by the granting of the motion. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Plaintiffs Joseph Fee, Laura Rees, And Nancy Fee'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KATHRYN B. MEADOWS, TEL. NO. 1-619-231-9460 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. CYRUS TABORI, TEL. NO. 1-408-288-9700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiffs Joseph Fee, Laura Rees, And Nancy Fee'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint seeking punitive damages is granted. The motion was timely filed under CCP 425.13 and plaintiffs have adduced sufficient evidence to show a substantial probability of prevailing on their request for punitive damages. The court does not weigh the evidence. (College Hospital Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1994) 8 Cal.4th 704, 719, n.6.) Plaintiff can amend to seek punitives on the IIED/Elder Abuse cause of action. Plaintiff cannot amend to seek punitives on the fraud/misrepresentation cause of action. The court denied the motion to amend to allege punitive damage on the Intentional Misrepresentation against SWBH and denied the motion to amend to allege concealment against SWBH and grant the rest of the motion. The motion to amend to allege punitive damage on the Intentional Misrepresentation against Miss Pasalo is Granted and denied as to Concealment as to Miss Pasalo. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312.
Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR # 11332 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS ANDREW GIANG and PACIFIC INPATIENT MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied in its entirety. CCP 425.13 does not apply to an elder abuse cause of action, plaintiff can recover fees per Welfare and Institutions Code sec. 15657, and defendant cites no reason to strike the allegations regarding the Department of Health. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS ANDREW GIANG and PACIFIC INPATIENT MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the Third Amended Complaint is overruled. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support the elder abuse cause of action. (See 3rd Amended Complaint, par. 51). The court overrules the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. The claim survives pursuant to Cal. Welfare and Institutions Code sec. 15657 and plaintiff alleges sufficient facts regarding outrageous conduct. The court overrules as to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Plaintiff observed her mother drown in what appeared to be her own excrement. No special medical acumen is necessary to understand that injury. (See 3rd Amended Complaint, par. 67; Ochoa v. Super. Ct. (1985), 39 Cal.3d 159, 170-172.) The Court overrules as to causes of action 6, 7, and 8. The fraud claims are alleged with sufficient particularity. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ehg

Off Calendar Jan-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KATHRYN B. MEADOWS, TEL. NO. (619) 231-9460 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MONIQUE SHAMUN-KHASHO OF THE LAW FIRM OF SHEUERMAN, MARTINI & GARVIN, TEL. NO. (408) 288-9700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS & SONIA PASALO;
ATTY. CORINNA E. MEISSNER OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT DR. GIANG.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS and SONIA PASALO, L.V.N. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. Defendant's demurrer is overruled as it relates to Plaintiff's Second through Eighth, Tenth, and Eleventh causes of action. Defendant's demurrer is sustained with 15 DAYS leave to amend as it relates to Plaintiff's Ninth cause of action because Penal Code 368 does not provide a civil cause of action and it is unclear from Plaintiff's complaint if this cause of action is asserted as negligence per se. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. KATHRYN B. MEADOWS, TEL. NO. (619) 231-9460 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MONIQUE SHAMUN-KHASHO OF THE LAW FIRM OF SHEUERMAN, MARTINI & GARVIN, TEL. NO. (408) 288-9700 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS & SONIA PASALO;
ATTY. CORINNA E. MEISSNER OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT DR. GIANG.

RULING - DEFENDANTS SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS and SONIA PASALO, L.V.N.'s MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted in part with leave to amend and denied in part. Plaintiff's prayer for relief as it relates to their Second and Third causes of action improperly includes a request for punitive damages under Plaintiff's Third cause of action. Plaintiffs must first obtain a court order to amend their complaint to request punitive damages under their Third cause of action pursuant to California Code of Civil Procedure section 425.13. The remainder of Plaintiff's prayer for relief conforms to statutory requirements and Defendant cites no authority supporting its contention that California Department of Public Health investigations and citations are inadmissible in pleadings. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
